This enables: 1) Current ownership query: WHERE validTo IS NULL. 2) Historical ownership at date: WHERE validFrom <= date AND (validTo IS NULL OR validTo > date). 3) Ownership audit: full history preserved, never deleted. When transferOwnership() occurs, current record gets validTo = now, new record created with validFrom = now. This temporal pattern supports regulatory requirements for ownership history and enables point-in-time reporting.

questions.question: How does the inventory upload session manage processing states?
questions.answer: EnumInventoryUploadStatus tracks session state: CREATED, VALIDATING, PROCESSING, COMPLETED, FAILED, CANCELLED. EnumInventoryProcessingStatus tracks record state: PENDING, VALIDATING, VALIDATED, PROCESSING, PROCESSED, FAILED, SKIPPED. Sessions flow: CREATED -> VALIDATING (storage locations, business entities, duplicates) -> PROCESSING (commit changes) -> COMPLETED/FAILED. Individual InventoryUploadStaging records have their own status progression. DataValidationService orchestrates phases with skip options. Failed records are marked FAILED with error details; session can still complete if enough records succeed.

questions.question: What business rules govern EnumPurchaseOrderStatus transitions?
questions.answer: EnumPurchaseOrderStatus tracks purchase order lifecycle: 1) Planned - PO created but not committed to vendor. 2) Committed - PO sent to vendor, awaiting execution. 3) Initiated - vendor confirmed, execution starting. 4) InProgress - barrels being received/filled. 5) Complete - all expected barrels received. Transitions: Planned -> Committed (approval), Committed -> Initiated (vendor confirm), Initiated -> InProgress (first barrel event), InProgress -> Complete (all barrels received). Complete POs cannot be modified. Status affects inventory projections and customer billing.

questions.question: How does the system track lot-level vs barrel-level transactions?
questions.answer: GroupTransaction supports two tracking modes via TransactionTrackingMode: 1) BARREL_LEVEL - affectedBarrelIds array populated with individual barrel IDs. BarrelEvent created for each barrel. Used for barrel-specific operations. 2) LOT_LEVEL - no individual barrel tracking. CreateLotLevelTransferInput used with quantity and unitType (PROOF_GALLONS, WINE_GALLONS, BARRELS, COUNT). LotLevelTransactionMetadata stores quantityChange, unitType, beforeQuantities, afterQuantities. Used for bulk liquid transfers where individual barrels aren't tracked. Both create FinancialTransaction records for accounting.

questions.question: What error handling strategy does LocationAlreadyOccupiedException implement?
questions.answer: LocationAlreadyOccupiedException (TEC-1173) stores comprehensive error context that survives Prisma transaction boundaries: barrelSN, locationString, occupyingBarrelSN, occupyingBarrelId, eventType, operator, deviceName, inboxDeviceName, inboxId, warehouseJobId, originalPayload. This enables error handlers outside the failed transaction to: 1) Log complete context for troubleshooting. 2) Create DeviceSyncError records with full details. 3) Provide actionable error messages to operators. The exception is thrown with CONFLICT status code when attempting to place a barrel in an already-occupied storage location.

questions.question: How does the approval workflow integrate with reconciliation?
questions.answer: When createReconciliation() is called with requiresApproval=true, it creates an ApprovalRequest linked to the ReconciliationRecord. ApprovalRequest has: requestType='RECONCILIATION', title, description, requestedBy, priority, estimatedImpact, requestData (contains reconciliationId and discrepancies). ApprovalResponse tracks approver decisions: PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, DELEGATED. ApprovalComment allows discussion. EnumApprovalRequestStatus (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED, EXPIRED) tracks overall status. High-impact reconciliations may require multiple approvers based on configuration.

questions.question: What is the purpose of EnumJobCreatorSource and how does it affect job behavior?
questions.answer: EnumJobCreatorSource tracks job origin: 1) WEB_UI - created manually via web interface by operator. 2) ANDROID_DEVICE - created from Android device scan during warehouse operations. 3) API - created via external API integration. 4) SYSTEM - auto-created by system (scheduled jobs, triggered workflows). 5) TEMPLATE - created from job template configuration. Source affects: default priority assignment, notification routing, audit trail context, retry policies. Android-created jobs may have different validation rules since they're created in the field. System jobs may bypass certain approval requirements.

questions.question: How does the financial transaction system track inventory movements?
questions.answer: FinancialTransaction records financial impact of inventory changes. FinancialTransactionSource categorizes: OWNERSHIP_TRANSACTION, TRANSFER_EVENT, MANUAL_ADJUSTMENT, PHYSICAL_COUNT, SYSTEM_CORRECTION, DAMAGE_WRITE_OFF, FOUND_INVENTORY, ROLLBACK, RECONCILIATION. EnumFinancialDirection (IN/OUT) tracks direction. FinancialUnitType (PROOF_GALLONS, WINE_GALLONS, BARRELS, COUNT, OTHER) specifies measurement. Transactions link to GroupTransaction, OwnershipTransaction, or other source entities. This enables: customer balance tracking, inventory valuation, regulatory reporting (TTB), and financial reconciliation.

questions.question: What constraints govern EnumTransferContainerType and its business significance?
questions.answer: EnumTransferContainerType specifies physical container for transfers: 1) LargeBarrel - standard 53-gallon whiskey barrel. 2) SmallBarrel - smaller format (5-15 gallon). 3) Tanker - bulk liquid transport (thousands of gallons). 4) Ss275 - 275-gallon stainless steel tote. 5) Ibc275 - 275-gallon intermediate bulk container. Container type affects: volume calculations, shipping logistics, insurance requirements, handling procedures. Tanker transfers require TANKER_LOAD/TANKER_UNLOAD job types. Barrel transfers use individual barrel tracking; bulk containers use lot-level tracking with volume measurements.

questions.question: How does the device sync error resolution system work?
questions.answer: EnumDeviceSyncErrorStatus tracks error state: RETRY (awaiting retry), RESOLVED (fixed), UNRESOLVED (needs manual intervention), REJECTED (not fixable). EnumDeviceSyncErrorResolutionType documents how resolved: SINGLE_RESOLUTION (individual fix), BULK_RESOLUTION (batch fix), AUTOMATED_RETRY_RESOLUTION (system retry succeeded). ResolutionPath defines resolution approach: ACCEPT (accept as-is), RESOLVE (manual fix applied), ACKNOWLEDGE (noted without fix), REJECT (invalid data). AndroidSyncInboxService tracks timeout patterns and provides getTransactionTimeoutRecords() for specific error recovery. Error context preserved via DeviceSyncError entity.

questions.question: What business rules govern lot whiskey type classification?
questions.answer: EnumLotWhiskeyType classifies lot contents: Bourbon, Rye, Malt, Wheat, Corn, Blended, LightWhiskey, Other, Unknown. Classification affects: TTB reporting categories, recipe matching, customer ordering, inventory segregation. Lot.WhiskeyType is a relation to WhiskeyType table (not enum directly) allowing custom types. LotService.transformLotForMES() converts WhiskeyType relation to MES enum via mapWhiskeyTypeCodeToEnum() for system integration. Classification typically determined by recipe grain bill percentages (e.g., 51%+ corn for bourbon).

questions.question: How does the warehouse type affect storage location structure?
questions.answer: EnumWarehouseTypeField distinguishes warehouse types: 1) Rick - traditional rick houses with floor/tier/rick/position hierarchy. StorageLocation uses EnumStorageLocationRick (A, B, C... for rick identifiers). 2) Palletized - pallet-based storage without traditional rick structure. Affects: storage location address format, capacity calculations, placement algorithms, navigation for operators. StorageLocation schema accommodates both with optional rick field. Warehouse.warehouseType determines valid location patterns. Different UIs and reports for each type.

questions.question: What is the purpose of bulk operation status tracking?
questions.answer: EnumBulkOperationStatus tracks multi-record operations: PENDING (queued), RUNNING (executing), COMPLETED (all succeeded), FAILED (all failed), CANCELLED (user cancelled), PARTIAL_SUCCESS (some succeeded). BulkOperation records: operationType, totalRecords, processedRecords, failedRecords, successRecords, errorSummary, executedBy, correlationId. Used by bulk ownership transfers, inventory updates, reconciliation batches. Enables: progress tracking, partial rollback, error reporting per record, resume capability. BulkOwnershipOperationResponse returns individual success/error arrays.

questions.question: How does inventory check staging manage the review workflow?
questions.answer: InventoryCheckStaging records barrel scans with InventoryCheckStagingStatus: PENDING (received), PROCESSING (being validated), READY (validation complete), APPROVED (passed review), REJECTED (failed review), COMMITTED (applied to inventory), ERROR (system error). InventoryCheckIssue links issues to staging records. The processor auto-transitions job from PENDING to IN_PROGRESS on first scan. Scans are validated against expected barrel locations. Discrepancies create issues for manual review. Approved records trigger barrel location updates; rejected records are logged but not applied.

questions.question: What constraints prevent barrel from being assigned to non-existent lot?
questions.answer: The populate_barrel_details() SQL function ensures lot exists before barrel creation: Step 5 first searches Lot table by lotNumber, creates new lot if not found (with createdBy, updatedBy from p_entry_by parameter). Only after v_lot_id is obtained does Step 7 create/update barrel with lotId. If lot handling fails, v_error_message is set and barrel creation is skipped. The Barrel.lotId foreign key references Lot.id, and Prisma enforces referential integrity. This prevents orphaned barrels and ensures complete lot-barrel relationships for inventory tracking.

questions.question: How does the comment system support different entity types?
questions.answer: EnumCommentableEntity defines entities supporting comments: INVENTORY_CHECK_STAGING, WAREHOUSE_JOB, BARREL, BARREL_EVENT, TRANSFER, CUSTOMER, LOT, PRODUCTION_ORDER, SCHEDULE_ORDER, APPROVAL_REQUEST, DEVICE_SYNC_ERROR, RECONCILIATION_RECORD, INVENTORY_UPLOAD_SESSION, INVENTORY_UPLOAD_STAGING, CHANGELOG_ENTRY. EnumCommentType categorizes: COMMENT, SYSTEM, APPROVAL, REJECTION, ESCALATION, GENERAL, QUESTION, ISSUE, RESOLUTION, INSTRUCTION, AUDIT, INVESTIGATION, WARNING. Comments have EnumCommentPriority, EnumCommentVisibility (PUBLIC, INTERNAL, RESTRICTED, PRIVATE), EnumContentType (TEXT, MARKDOWN, JSON, HTML). This polymorphic design enables consistent commenting across the application.

questions.question: What business rules govern the serial number uniqueness constraint?
questions.answer: Barrel serial number uniqueness is enforced via partial index (per schema.prisma comments): 'Unique constraint enforced via partial index (allows duplicates when isDuplicateOverride = true)'. This enables: 1) Normal case: serial numbers are unique across all barrels. 2) Override case: legitimate duplicates allowed when explicitly flagged (e.g., barrel re-entry after regauge). DuplicateBarrelException carries context including normalizedSerial for lot variation duplicates where different lot number formats resolve to same canonical lot. httpResponseBarrelSerialNumberDuplicatedException provides simple duplicate error for API responses.

questions.question: How does the lot synchronization with MES work?
questions.answer: LotService publishes lot events to MES via RabbitMQ: createLot(), updateLot(), deleteLot() each call publishLotEvent() after database operation. Event message format: {event_type, data, recordName: 'Lot', sourceSystem: 'WMS'}. transformLotForMES() converts WMS format to MES-compatible format: 1) Maps WhiskeyType relation to MES enum via mapWhiskeyTypeCodeToEnum(). 2) Converts relation globalIds for recipe, productionOrder, furnishingCustomer. 3) Removes WMS-specific fields. Events published to WhMessageBrokerTopics.WhWhk01Distillery01Lot. Failures are logged but don't fail the database operation.

questions.question: What constraints govern the message queue status lifecycle?
questions.answer: EnumMessageQueueStatus tracks message processing: values likely include PENDING (awaiting processing), PROCESSING (currently handled), COMPLETED (successfully processed), FAILED (error occurred), DEAD_LETTER (moved to dead letter queue after max retries). AndroidSyncInbox uses sync boolean and error string for similar tracking. RabbitMQProducerService.emitMessage() publishes to topic; failures logged but don't fail calling operation. This decoupled approach ensures core WMS operations succeed even if messaging infrastructure is unavailable.

questions.question: How does inheritance ownership preserve parent relationship?
questions.answer: BarrelOwnershipService.establishInheritance() creates child ownership records that preserve parent relationship: 1) Gets all active ownerships for parentCustomerId. 2) For each ownership, creates new BarrelOwnership with: ownershipType=InheritedChild, inheritedFromCustomerId=parentCustomerId, inheritanceDate=now. 3) Records INHERITANCE transaction with fromCustomerId/toCustomerId. Original parent ownership remains active (inherited child is additional, not replacement). Child customer can see and operate on inherited barrels while maintaining clear lineage. Used for corporate structures where subsidiary inherits parent's inventory rights.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the barrel fill level enumeration?
questions.answer: EnumBarrelFillLevel indicates barrel contents: Full (100% capacity), ThreeQuarter (75%), Half (50%), Quarter (25%), Empty (0%), Unknown (not measured). Fill level is updated during: initial fill (Full), sampling (reduced), dumping operations (Empty), regauging (measured). Affects: inventory calculations (proof gallons = fill level * capacity * proof), aging projections, insurance valuations. Empty barrels remain tracked for cooperage inventory and potential refill. Quality events may reference fill level for context.

questions.question: How does the sample type result type affect sample processing?
questions.answer: EnumSampleTypeTypeResultType at schema.prisma defines only TWO values: Boolean, Numeric. The actual implementation uses a simpler type system where sample result types are categorized as either Boolean (binary pass/fail) or Numeric (continuous value measurement). Sample analysis results are tracked separately in the Sample.sampleResult field (Boolean) and Sample.sampleValue field (Float), not through result type categorization. This differs from a predicted status-based approach (Pass, Fail, Pending, etc.).

questions.question: What business logic governs the transfer date cascade?
questions.answer: Transfer dates follow priority cascade: dateReceived (when goods physically arrived) -> dateLoaded (when loaded onto transport) -> dateEntered (when recorded in system) -> createdAt (record creation timestamp). TransferService uses COALESCE(dateReceived, dateLoaded, dateEntered, createdAt) for sorting and display. This cascade ensures: 1) Most relevant date shown (physical event preferred over administrative). 2) Consistent sorting across transfers with different date fields populated. 3) Never null result for date display. Frontend getTransferDate() function mirrors this logic for UI consistency.

questions.question: How does the audit log system track entity changes?
questions.answer: EnumAuditOperationType defines operations: Create, Update, Delete, UPDATE (duplicate likely legacy). EnumAuditEntityType enumerates auditable entities: User, StorageLocation, Barrel, Sample, Customer, SalesOrder, Item, Lot, WeightLog, etc. EnumAuditOperationResult: success, failure. AuditService.createAuditLog() records: operationType, entityType, entityId, userId, correlationId, beforeState, afterState, operationDetails. Used throughout services for compliance (TTB regulations require complete audit trail). ReconciliationService creates audit logs for reconciliation operations. Audit reports support multiple formats and date ranges.

questions.question: What constraints prevent overlapping barrel ownerships?
questions.answer: The ownership system prevents overlapping active ownerships through: 1) Single owner check: When creating sole ownership, system verifies no existing active ownership (validTo IS NULL) exists. 2) Joint ownership: All joint owners must be created atomically with single jointOwnershipGroupId, percentages summing to 100%. 3) Transfer operations: Old ownership's validTo is set before new ownership created. 4) Aggregate validation: createOwnership() checks currentOwnershipTotal + newPercentage <= 100 within transaction. This ensures at any point in time, total ownership percentages for a barrel equal exactly 100%.

questions.question: How does the resolution system suggest serial numbers?
questions.answer: Resolution services provide serial number suggestions for error correction: predict-next-9xxx-serial.dto.ts handles 9xxx-series temporary serials (e.g., 24E01A.01.9001 with nextNumber 9001-9999 range). suggest-serial-number.dto.ts returns SerialNumberSuggestionDto with suggestedSerial, confidence score (0-100), confidenceLevel (high/medium/low), lotAnalysis with barrelCount and percentage, reasoning, alternatives with multiple suggestions, and adjacentBarrels for visualization. The BarrelMatchingService matches scanned data against existing barrels considering: lot variations via LotVariationService and distance weights. The prediction algorithm uses weights defined in prediction-weights.dto.ts: adjacentLotsWithSequence (60%), currentJobLots (20%), horizontalPositionLots (20%).

questions.question: What business rules govern schedule day operations?
questions.answer: EnumScheduleDayName enumerates working days: Monday through Saturday (no Sunday - distillery operations follow 6-day week). Schedule, ScheduleDay, ScheduleOrder models manage production scheduling. ManhourConfiguration uses barrels-filled-aggregate.dto.ts to calculate labor hours needed. Schedules link to ProductionOrder and StaffSchedule for resource allocation. This affects: fill run planning, transfer scheduling, inventory check timing. Weekend closure means Saturday is last day for week's operations; Monday starts new production week.

questions.question: How does the printer automation system track print jobs?
questions.answer: EnumPrinterStatus: likely Online, Offline, Error, Maintenance. EnumAutomationPrintJobStatus: likely Queued, Printing, Completed, Failed, Cancelled. Printer model tracks physical printers with status. AutomationPrintJob tracks label print requests. LabelPrinted and LabelReprinted events (EnumEventTypeName) are recorded for audit trail. printer.dto.ts, printer-config-sync.dto.ts, printer-full-sync.dto.ts manage printer configuration. This enables: automated label printing on barrel fill, reprint on demand, tracking of printed vs unprinted barrels for compliance.

questions.question: What validation ensures storage location capacity is not exceeded?
questions.answer: httpResponseLackofPositionsAvailableException throws when 'Not enough positions available!' This occurs when attempting to place barrels in a location that's already full. StorageLocation has position field indicating capacity. Barrel placement checks available positions before assignment. The exception uses BAD_REQUEST status. This prevents: overfilling storage locations, unsafe stacking, inventory tracking errors. Palletized vs Rick warehouses have different capacity calculations based on warehouseType.

questions.question: How does the config sync system manage distributed settings?
questions.answer: EnumConfigSyncType, EnumConfigSyncAction, EnumConfigSyncStatus manage configuration synchronization. printer-config-sync.dto.ts handles printer settings distribution. EnumLogoSyncStatus, EnumLogoSyncAction, EnumLogoSyncRequestStatus manage customer logo distribution. Full sync (EnumFullSyncStatus) handles complete configuration refresh. This enables: consistent settings across Android devices, centralized logo management, version-controlled configuration. Sync failures create retry records for eventual consistency.

questions.question: What constraints govern lot audit actions?
questions.answer: EnumLotAuditAction: Create, Update, Delete - tracks changes to Lot records. LotAuditService (injected into GroupTransactionService) records lot-level changes. EnumBarrelAuditAction mirrors for barrel changes. Audit actions recorded with: entityId, operationType, userId, timestamp, beforeState, afterState. Lot audits are critical for: TTB compliance (federal regulations require complete lot history), customer reporting, production traceability. All lot modifications (bblTotal, status, quantities) generate audit records.

questions.question: How does the email log system track notification delivery?
questions.answer: EnumEmailLogStatus: INITIALIZED (email created, not yet sent), SENT (successfully delivered). Email logs track: recipient, subject, content, status, timestamps. Used for: customer notifications, approval requests, error alerts. Integration with notification system ensures: delivery confirmation, retry capability for failed sends, audit trail of communications. Status transitions: INITIALIZED -> SENT on successful delivery; failed sends remain INITIALIZED with error logged.

questions.question: What business rules govern the feedback collection system?
questions.answer: EnumFeedbackType defines: FEATURE_REQUEST, BUG_REPORT, IMPROVEMENT (not 'Bug', 'Feature', 'Improvement'). feedback.dto.ts (CreateFeedbackRequestDto) defines feedback submission with type, title (5-200 chars), description, authorId, authorEmail, authorName, changelogEntryId, and structured fields (FeatureRequestFieldsDto, BugReportFieldsDto, ImprovementFieldsDto). Feedback collected from web interface with author identification. Feedback may trigger: changelog entries (via changelogEntryId link), linear issue creation (linearIssueId field). Anonymous feedback is NOT explicitly supported - all author fields are required.

questions.question: How does the group transaction status machine work?
questions.answer: EnumGroupTransactionStatus: PENDING (created, not executed), IN_PROGRESS (executing), COMPLETED (success), FAILED (error), REVERSED (undone), CANCELLED (abandoned). GroupTransactionService.executeTransaction(): 1) Validates status=PENDING. 2) Updates to IN_PROGRESS. 3) Executes based on transactionType. 4) On success: COMPLETED with completedAt. 5) On failure: FAILED with error in metadata. REVERSED status used for transaction rollback. CANCELLED for user-initiated abort. Status machine prevents: re-execution of completed transactions, execution of failed transactions without intervention.

questions.question: What invariants must hold for the barrel-lot relationship?
questions.answer: Every barrel must have a valid lotId (foreign key to Lot). populate_barrel_details() ensures lot exists or creates it before barrel. Barrel.serialNumber format typically includes lot number (e.g., '24A001.0478'). Lot.bblTotal should equal count of barrels with that lotId (maintained by events). Barrels in lot share: whiskeyType, recipe, fillDate (approximately). These invariants ensure: accurate lot-level reporting, proper inventory aggregation, regulatory compliance for batch tracking. Orphaned barrels (no lot) are invalid states.

questions.question: How does the trust mode configuration affect validation?
questions.answer: trust-mode-config.dto.ts defines trust mode settings affecting validation strictness. Trust mode may allow: automatic barrel creation on scan (vs requiring pre-registration), auto-creation of lots (validate_business_entities_with_auto_lot_creation), relaxed location validation. Higher trust = faster operations but risk of data quality issues. Lower trust = more validation gates but slower throughput. Configuration per warehouse/operation type allows: pilot warehouse with strict mode, established warehouse with trusted mode. Affects DataValidationService option handling.

questions.question: What constraints govern warehouse job priority?
questions.answer: EnumWarehouseJobPriority: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, URGENT. Priority affects: job queue ordering, resource allocation, notification urgency. URGENT jobs may: bypass normal queue, trigger immediate alerts, require expedited completion. Priority assigned based on: EnumJobCreatorSource (system jobs may be higher), transfer deadlines, customer importance, inventory criticality. Jobs can be re-prioritized during execution. Priority history tracked for SLA compliance. URGENT typically reserved for safety or regulatory issues.

questions.question: How does the changelog system track application changes?
questions.answer: changelog.dto.ts defines changelog entry structure. ChangelogEntry is EnumCommentableEntity (supports comments). Changelog tracks: version releases, feature additions, bug fixes, breaking changes. EnumCommentType includes GENERAL and SYSTEM for changelog annotations. Used for: user communication, support reference, audit compliance. Changelog entries may link to: git commits, issue trackers, affected entities. Comments on changelog enable: user feedback, clarification requests, deployment notes.

questions.question: What business rules prevent modification of completed transactions?
questions.answer: GroupTransactionService.executeTransaction() validates status === PENDING before execution; throws BadRequestException('Cannot execute transaction in status: {status}') otherwise. Completed transactions have immutable affectedBarrelIds and metadata. To correct completed transactions: create new REVERSAL transaction or use reconciliation system. This ensures: audit trail integrity, prevents double-counting, maintains financial accuracy. FAILED transactions can be retried after fixing underlying issue. Status check happens inside $transaction to prevent race conditions.

questions.question: How does the finance service handle inventory adjustments?
questions.answer: inventory-adjustment.dto.ts defines adjustment input with InventoryUnitType (mirrors FinancialUnitType: PROOF_GALLONS, WINE_GALLONS, BARRELS, COUNT). FinancialTransaction created with FinancialTransactionSource.MANUAL_ADJUSTMENT or PHYSICAL_COUNT. finance-request.dto.ts handles report generation. EnumFinancialDirection (IN/OUT) tracks adjustment direction. Adjustments require: reason documentation, approval for large amounts, audit trail. Used for: physical count corrections, damage write-offs (DAMAGE_WRITE_OFF), found inventory (FOUND_INVENTORY).

questions.question: What validation prevents invalid customer data references?
questions.answer: Customer data stored in JSON 'data' field with customerCode, name, etc. populate_barrel_details() validates: SELECT id FROM Customer WHERE data->>'code' = v_customer_code. If not found: v_error_message = 'Customer not found for code: ' || v_customer_code. TransferService extracts customerName via JSON: c.data::jsonb->>'name' or c.data::jsonb->>'customerName'. Invalid customer references prevent: barrel creation (customer required for ownership), transfer completion, financial transactions. Customer validation ensures data integrity for ownership tracking.

questions.question: How does the search system provide cross-entity results?
questions.answer: search-result.dto.ts defines unified search result format. Search covers EnumAuditEntityType entities: Barrel, Lot, Customer, Transfer, etc. Results include: entityType, entityId, displayName, matchedField, relevanceScore. Search implementation uses: PostgreSQL full-text search, pattern matching on serial numbers, JSON field queries for customer data. Unified search enables: quick navigation, cross-reference discovery, operator efficiency. Results filtered by user permissions and entity visibility.

questions.question: What constraints govern the report generation system?
questions.answer: ReportType enumerates: CURRENT_INVENTORY, DAILY/WEEKLY/MONTHLY/QUARTERLY/ANNUAL_INVENTORY, TRANSACTION_SUMMARY/DETAIL, GROUP_TRANSACTION_SUMMARY, INVENTORY_AGING, WAREHOUSE_UTILIZATION, CUSTOMER_ALLOCATION, LOT_TRACKING, CUSTOM. ReportFormat: JSON, CSV, EXCEL, PDF, HTML. ReportStatus: PENDING, GENERATING, COMPLETED, FAILED, SCHEDULED. finance-report-templates.dto.ts defines report templates. Reports support: date range filters, entity filters, format selection. Large reports run asynchronously with status tracking. Reports used for: TTB compliance, customer statements, management dashboards.

questions.question: How does the barrel aggregation system provide inventory summaries?
questions.answer: BarrelAggregates module provides: barrel-oem-count.dto.ts (count by cooperage), barrel-period-count.dto.ts (count by time period), barrel-period-oem-count.dto.ts (combined), barrel-recipe-count.dto.ts (count by recipe), barrel-size-count.dto.ts (count by barrel size). inventory-aggregates.dto.ts combines all dimensions. location-aggregate.dto.ts provides warehouse utilization. inventory-by-recipe.dto.ts supports blend planning. These aggregations enable: dashboard KPIs, capacity planning, customer reporting, production scheduling without querying every barrel.

questions.question: What business rules govern the chat history system?
questions.answer: chat-history.dto.ts defines conversation storage with ChatMessageRole enum: USER, ASSISTANT (no 'System' role). Messages stored with: id, createdAt, role (USER|ASSISTANT), content, metadata. Conversations with: id, createdAt, updatedAt, userId, title (optional, 255 char max), lastMessageAt, metadata, soft-delete support (deletedAt field). Chat history used by AI agent for context. Agent module (agent-chat.dto.ts, agent-stream-events.dto.ts) handles AI interactions with ConversationMessageDto (role: 'user'|'assistant'), AgentChatRequestDto for requests, and AgentStartEvent/AgentCancelEvent for WebSocket streaming. Message metadata includes response time, agent iterations, token usage, tools used.

questions.question: How does the purchase order line item system work?
questions.answer: purchase-order-line-item.dto.ts defines line items within PurchaseOrder. purchase-order-with-line-items.dto.ts includes full order with items. purchase-order-with-receipt.dto.ts tracks receiving against order. Line items specify: itemNumber, quantity, unit price, expected delivery. Receipt matching validates: received quantity vs ordered, barrel serials against expected lot. EnumPurchaseOrderStatus tracks overall order progress. Partial receipts update line item received quantities. This enables: vendor management, receiving verification, cost tracking.

questions.question: What constraints govern the device sync diagnostics system?
questions.answer: android-sync-diagnostics.dto.ts provides sync health metrics. device-sync-error-diagnostics.dto.ts analyzes error patterns. AndroidSyncInboxService provides: getTotalRecords(), getUnsyncedRecords(), getTransactionTimeoutRecords(), getTimeoutErrorStats(). Diagnostics include: totalTimeoutErrors, uniqueDevices, errorsByDevice. testTransactionTimeout() validates timeout configuration. These diagnostics enable: proactive error detection, device health monitoring, sync queue management. High error rates trigger: alerts, automatic retry, escalation to support.

questions.question: How does the whiskey type management work?
questions.answer: WhiskeyType entity (separate from enum) allows custom types. create-whiskey-type.dto.ts, update-whiskey-type.dto.ts manage CRUD with fields: code (max 10 chars, unique), name, description, category, active (default true), isTTBDefinition (default false), and ttbWhiskeyTypeId (for parent category references). Lot.whiskeyTypeId references WhiskeyType.id. mapWhiskeyTypeCodeToEnum() in lot service converts code to EnumLotWhiskeyType for MES sync (e.g., 'RB' -> 'RyeBourbon'). This design allows: custom whiskey types per distillery, TTB compliance tracking, hierarchical type categories, standard types for reporting, and backward compatibility with enum-based systems.

questions.question: What business rules govern the barrel pivot audit system?
questions.answer: barrel-pivot-filter.dto.ts defines filters: date ranges, customers, lots, dispositions, locations. barrel-pivot-response.dto.ts returns aggregated data. Pivot enables: cross-tabulation of barrel attributes, trend analysis, comparison reporting. Audit dashboard (audit-filter.dto.ts, audit-response.dto.ts) provides: operation history, user activity, entity changes. Pivot queries optimize for: large datasets, multiple dimensions, time-series analysis. Used for: management reporting, compliance audits, operational insights.

questions.question: How does the aggregated storage entries system work?
questions.answer: aggregated-storage-entries-response.dto.ts returns storage summary. aggregated-storage-entry.dto.ts defines per-location metrics. breakdown.dto.ts provides detailed breakdown. Aggregation calculates: occupied positions, available capacity, barrel counts by location. Used for: warehouse map visualization, capacity planning, placement optimization. Breakdown enables: drill-down from summary to detail, location-specific views, utilization heatmaps. Data refreshed on: barrel placement, relocation, removal events.

questions.question: What validation prevents barrel creation without required location?
questions.answer: httpResponseLocationBodyRequiredException throws 'Storage Location body is required!' when barrel creation attempts without location specification. Location can be: StorageLocation (for ricked barrels) or HoldingLocation (for staging). BarrelLocationConflictException prevents both simultaneously. httpResponseBarrelIDExistsException throws when 'Storage Location is occupied!' - the target location already has a barrel. These validations ensure: every barrel is tracked, no location conflicts, complete location history for audit.

questions.question: How does the inventory upload reconciliation system work?
questions.answer: reconciliation-summary.dto.ts provides upload reconciliation metrics. session-management.dto.ts, session-recovery.dto.ts handle session lifecycle. Reconciliation compares: uploaded barrels vs existing inventory, expected vs actual locations, customer assignments. InventoryUploadReconciliationDuplicateRemoved, InventoryUploadDisplaced events track conflict resolution. High discrepancy counts may: halt processing, require approval, trigger investigation. This ensures: upload accuracy, prevents data corruption, maintains inventory integrity during bulk updates.

questions.question: What constraints govern the lot with barrel count reporting?
questions.answer: lot-with-barrel-count.dto.ts returns lots with computed barrel count. lot-with-whiskey-type.dto.ts includes type classification. Computed count should equal: Barrel.count WHERE lotId = lot.id AND disposition IN (active states). Count used for: lot completion tracking, production reporting, inventory valuation. Discrepancy between Lot.bblTotal and actual count triggers: ReconciliationRecord creation, investigation workflow, data correction process. Reports group lots by: status, whiskeyType, customer, date range.

questions.question: How does the manhour configuration affect fill operations?
questions.answer: barrels-filled-aggregate.dto.ts provides fill metrics for labor planning. barrels-filled-query.dto.ts defines query parameters: date range, recipe, operator. ManhourConfiguration referenced in EnumAuditEntityType for tracking changes. Configuration defines: barrels per hour rate, crew size requirements, setup/cleanup time. Aggregates enable: labor cost calculation, efficiency tracking, schedule optimization. Fill run planning uses: expected barrel count, configured rate, available crew hours.

questions.question: What business rules govern customer logo synchronization?
questions.answer: create-logo.dto.ts, update-logo.dto.ts manage customer logos. customer-logo.dto.ts defines logo entity. EnumLogoSyncStatus, EnumLogoSyncAction, EnumLogoSyncRequestStatus track synchronization. Logos sync to: Android devices for label printing, web interface for branding, reports for customer statements. Sync ensures: consistent branding, current logo versions, offline availability. Logo changes trigger: sync requests to all devices, print template updates, cache invalidation.

questions.question: How does the warehouse job aggregated staging work?
questions.answer: get-aggregated-staging-records.dto.ts provides staging summary for warehouse jobs. process-barrel-fill-job.dto.ts, process-bottling-job.dto.ts, process-tanker-load-job.dto.ts define job-specific processing. Aggregation includes: total records, by status counts, issue breakdown. Job status progresses based on staging completion. SCANNING_COMPLETE status requires: all expected scans received, staging records processed, issues flagged. Aggregation enables: progress tracking, bottleneck identification, job completion estimation.

questions.question: What validation occurs during inventory upload CSV processing?
questions.answer: upload-csv.dto.ts defines CSV field mapping. validation.dto.ts defines validation result types. DataValidationService.validateSchemaCompliance() uses Zod schema validation on staging rows. CSV rows mapped to: Notes, Customer, Warehouse, Floor, Tier, Rick, Position, Lot#, Mashbill, Barrel#. Validation phases: 1) Schema compliance (field types/formats). 2) Storage location existence. 3) Business entity validity (lots, customers). 4) Duplicate detection. Invalid rows marked with error details; valid rows proceed to processing.

questions.question: How does the bulk barrel operations system work?
questions.answer: bulk-barrel-update.dto.ts defines update input. bulk-operation.dto.ts defines operation structure. filter-bulk-barrels.dto.ts specifies selection criteria. bulk-operation-response.dto.ts returns results. Operations include: bulk disposition change, bulk customer assignment, bulk location update. Filter criteria: barrelIds, customerIds, recipes, lotIds, serialNumbers, date ranges, sizes, toasts, chars. buildBarrelFilterWhere() converts filter to Prisma WHERE clause. Results track: success count, failure count, individual errors.

questions.question: What constraints govern the approve record workflow?
questions.answer: approve-record.dto.ts defines approval input: decision (APPROVED/REJECTED/NEEDS_REVIEW), comments, reviewedBy. ApprovalDecision enum used for staging records. InventoryCheckStagingStatus includes APPROVED, REJECTED states. Approval transitions staging from READY -> APPROVED (commit to inventory) or REJECTED (discard). Rejected records require: reason documentation, potential re-scan. Multiple approvers may be required for: high-value items, discrepancy resolution, cross-department operations.

questions.question: How does the storage location with holding locations system work?
questions.answer: storage-location-with-holding-locations.dto.ts defines relation between ricked storage and holding areas. StorageLocation represents permanent ricked position. HoldingLocation represents temporary staging area. Barrels transition: HoldingLocation -> StorageLocation (after ricking), StorageLocation -> HoldingLocation (for transfer prep). One-to-many relationship allows multiple holding areas per storage zone. This supports: staging workflows, transfer consolidation, inventory movement tracking.

questions.question: What business rules govern the inventory upload error reporting?
questions.answer: error-reporting.dto.ts defines ErrorCategory: STORAGE, ENTITY, INTEGRITY, SYSTEM. phase-tracking.dto.ts tracks validation phase progress. ErrorCategorizationService categorizes errors. ErrorStorageService persists errors. Errors include: rowNumber, field, value, context. Categories determine: resolution path, severity, responsible party. STORAGE errors: invalid locations. ENTITY errors: missing lots/customers. INTEGRITY errors: duplicates, constraint violations. SYSTEM errors: unexpected failures. Error reports enable: batch correction, pattern detection, process improvement.

questions.question: How does the create from Android scan workflow work?
questions.answer: create-from-android-scan.dto.ts defines job creation from device scan. AndroidSyncInbox receives scan data. If no warehouseJobId in scan, system may auto-create job. Job creation considers: eventType, location, operator. EnumJobCreatorSource.ANDROID_DEVICE marks origin. Auto-created jobs start PENDING, transition to IN_PROGRESS on first scan processing. This enables: field-initiated workflows, operator flexibility, reduced planning overhead. Scan context preserved for audit trail.

questions.question: What constraints govern pagination in barrel ownership queries?
questions.answer: BarrelOwnershipService respects MAX_TAKE and DEFAULT_TAKE from pagination.constants. findBarrelsForBulkOwnership() returns barrels ordered by serialNumber ascending. Bulk operations use filter-based selection rather than explicit pagination for completeness. For large result sets, consider: cursor-based pagination, batch processing, async operations. MAX_SORT_RECORDS (10000 in TransferService) shows safety limits for in-memory operations. Pagination ensures: API responsiveness, memory safety, consistent ordering.

questions.question: How does the transfer type mapper work for group transactions?
questions.answer: mapTransferTypeToGroupTransactionType() in groupTransaction service converts transfer types to group transaction types. TransferTypeMapping interface returns: transactionType (EnumGroupTransactionType), direction (EnumFinancialDirection). Mapping enables: consistent type handling, financial tracking, event creation. TRANSFER_IN maps to EnumGroupTransactionType.TRANSFER_IN with direction IN. TRANSFER_OUT maps similarly with direction OUT. This abstraction isolates transfer logic from transaction recording.

questions.question: What business rules govern the ownership ledger entries?
questions.answer: createOwnershipLedgerEntries() utility (imported in BarrelOwnershipService) creates financial records for ownership changes. Ledger entries track: barrelId, fromCustomer, toCustomer, percentage, date, transaction reference. Entries enable: customer statement generation, balance calculations, ownership history queries. Ledger maintains: running totals per customer, proof gallon valuations, cost basis tracking. Financial transactions link to ledger entries for complete audit trail.

questions.question: How does the inventory check reconciliation service determine critical issues?
questions.answer: inventory-check-reconciliation.service.ts tracks criticalIssueCount during staging processing. EnumDeviceSyncErrorLevel.CRITICAL flags severe issues. Critical issues include: barrel not found, location conflict, duplicate detection. Processor updates job with issueCount and criticalIssueCount. Critical threshold may: block job completion, require supervisor approval, trigger alerts. Issue severity determines: review priority, resolution workflow, escalation path. Non-critical issues may: auto-resolve, queue for batch review, allow proceeding with warning.

questions.question: What validation ensures warehouse job type matches operation?
questions.answer: GroupTransactionService.executeTransaction() switches on transactionType to call appropriate executor: LOT_ENTRY -> executeLotEntry(), TRANSFER_IN/OUT -> executeTransfer(), INTERNAL_MOVE -> executeInternalMove(). Unsupported types throw BadRequestException. WarehouseJob.jobType (EnumWarehouseJobType) similarly determines: expected events, staging requirements, completion criteria. Mismatched types prevent: wrong event creation, incorrect inventory updates, audit trail confusion. Type validation occurs at: job creation, transaction execution, event processing.

questions.question: How does the find by transfer ID system work for warehouse jobs?
questions.answer: find-by-transfer-id-response.dto.ts returns jobs associated with a transfer. WarehouseJob links to Transfer via: transferId field, primaryGroupTransactions relation. Query finds jobs where: transferId matches OR groupTransaction.transferId matches. Multiple jobs may exist for: complex transfers, multi-phase operations, retry attempts. Response includes: job status, staging progress, associated barrels. This enables: transfer-centric views, operation tracking, completion verification.

questions.question: What constraints govern context service usage in reconciliation?
questions.answer: ContextService injected into ReconciliationService provides: current user context, request correlation ID, session information. Context used for: createdBy/lastModifiedBy fields, audit trail userId, correlation tracking. Missing context handled gracefully with defaults. ContextService enables: user attribution without passing userId everywhere, request tracing, tenant isolation (if multi-tenant). Reconciliation operations always record: who initiated, when, correlation to source event.

questions.question: How does the decomposition strategy affect job creation?
questions.answer: DecompositionStrategy enum defines job splitting: BY_LOCATION (separate job per warehouse zone), BY_CUSTOMER (per customer's barrels), BY_LOT (per lot), BY_DATE_RANGE (by fill date periods), BY_QUANTITY (fixed batch sizes), CUSTOM (user-defined logic), INHERITED (child uses parent's strategy). Parent jobs decompose into child jobs for: parallel execution, progress tracking, partial completion. Child jobs maintain: parentJobId reference, inherited priority, aggregated progress. This enables: large operation management, progress visibility, failure isolation.

questions.question: What business rules govern the update warehouse job workflow?
questions.answer: update-warehouse-job.dto.ts defines updatable fields: title, description, priority, assignedTo, dueDate, notes. Status updates follow state machine rules. Cannot update: completed jobs (except notes), cancelled jobs. Priority changes may: reorder queue, trigger notifications, update SLA expectations. Assignment changes: notify new assignee, track handoff history. Due date changes: recalculate schedule impact, warn of conflicts. All updates create audit records.

questions.question: How does the safe transaction method handle timeouts?
questions.answer: PrismaService.$safeTransaction() (referenced in AndroidSyncInboxService) handles transaction timeouts: default 60-second timeout (vs old 5-second), configurable per-call (e.g., 70000ms for testing). testTransactionTimeout() validates configuration by running pg_sleep() within transaction. Transaction timeout errors include: 'Unable to start a transaction in the given time', 'Transaction already closed', 'timeout for this transaction was 5000 ms', 'Transaction not found'. getTransactionTimeoutRecords() finds affected sync records for retry. Extended timeouts prevent: false failures on slow operations, deadlock misattribution.

questions.question: What is the complete barrel lifecycle from fill to customer transfer?
questions.answer: Complete barrel lifecycle: 1) BARREL_FILL job creates barrel with disposition=Filled, event=Fill. 2) Barrel placed in HoldingLocation, disposition=InHolding, event=Entry. 3) Barrel ricked to StorageLocation, disposition=InStorage, event=Relocation. 4) BarrelOwnership created linking customer (ESTABLISHMENT transaction). 5) Periodic events: Sample, InventoryCheck, Quality, Inspection. 6) TRANSFER_OUT job initiated, barrel moved to HoldingLocation. 7) Transfer completed, disposition=TransferredOffsite or Sold, event=TransferOut. 8) OwnershipTransaction records transfer, FinancialTransaction records value movement. Each step creates BarrelEvent, audit trail, and potentially GroupTransaction for batch operations.

questions.question: Trace the complete data flow when an Android device scans a barrel QR code and submits it to the WMS. What happens from the moment the scan is received until the barrel's location is updated in the database?
questions.answer: The data flow is: 1) Android device sends scan data to AndroidSyncInbox via REST API, 2) The android-sync.processor.ts picks up the inbox record from the BullMQ queue, 3) The processor fetches the AndroidSyncInbox record using the deviceName and inboxId compound key, 4) BarrelEventService.createBarrelEventWithLocationAndLocationCheck() is called which routes to appropriate handler based on eventType (Entry, InventoryCheck, Relocation, etc.), 5) The service uses Prisma.$androidSyncTransaction for serializable isolation to prevent race conditions, 6) Barrel is looked up/created via findOrCreateBarrel with lot variation normalization, 7) Location is resolved (storage or holding), 8) BarrelEvent is created and barrel.locationId/holdingLocationId is updated, 9) WebSocket progress updates are broadcast via ProgressGateway.

questions.question: How does the inventory upload processing system handle large CSV files? Describe the complete data transformation pipeline from file upload to database persistence.
questions.answer: The flow is: 1) Frontend uploads CSV to inventory-upload endpoint, 2) File is parsed and rows are inserted into InventoryUploadStaging table with status 'pending', 3) A Bull job is queued to 'inventory-processing' queue via InventoryProcessingProcessor, 4) Processor processes in batches (configurable batchSize), calling inventoryProcessingService.processBatch(), 5) SQL function processes staging records: validates data, resolves lots via LotVariationService, creates/updates barrels, 6) Progress is tracked via ProgressTrackingService and broadcast via WebSocket ProgressGateway, 7) SafetyLimitsService monitors for resource limits and circuit breaker patterns, 8) DatabaseProgressService logs batch progress and events, 9) On completion, TrustModeProcessingService handles cutoff date processing if enabled.

questions.question: What is the complete authentication flow when a user accesses the WMS from the frontend? Trace the data from Azure AD login through to GraphQL query authorization.
questions.answer: The flow is: 1) Frontend redirects to Azure AD login using MSAL with AZURE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID, 2) User authenticates, Azure returns access token, 3) Frontend sends token in Authorization header with GraphQL requests, 4) GqlDefaultAuthGuard intercepts request, 5) AzureAdService.validateTokenAndGetUser() decodes JWT, validates claims (issuer, audience, tenant), 6) For API tokens (not Graph tokens), it uses OBO flow or falls back to handleGraphFallback(), 7) getUserInfoFromGraph() or getUserInfoByObjectId() retrieves user profile, 8) If OBO needed, msalApp.acquireTokenOnBehalfOf() exchanges token for Graph-scoped token, 9) User context is attached to request for downstream resolvers.

questions.question: How does the InventoryCheckStaging processor validate barrel scans and detect issues? Describe the complete validation pipeline including BAD_QR, NOLABEL, and duplicate detection.
questions.answer: The validation pipeline in InventoryCheckStagingProcessor.validateAndDetectIssues(): 1) Phase 0A checks for BAD_QR/NOLABEL patterns using regex (isBadQrScan, isNoLabelScan), creates CRITICAL issues requiring E&T intervention, 2) Phase 0B detects EMPTY_POSITION scans, checks if location has occupying barrel for displacement, 3) Phase 1 validates barrel existence - if not found, attempts lot resolution via resolveLotFromSerialNumber, checks for duplicate lot variations on canonical lot, 4) Phase 1B checks for duplicate barrels with same barrel number on canonical lot, 5) Phase 2 validates location - resolves via resolveLocationFromString, checks occupancy via checkLocationOccupied, 6) Phase 3 finds duplicate scans within same warehouse job via findDuplicateScans, 7) Phase 4 validates serial number format, 8) Issues are persisted to InventoryCheckIssue table with errorLevel (INFO/WARN/ERROR/CRITICAL).

questions.question: Explain the BullMQ queue configuration and how it prevents stalled jobs. What are the key settings and their rationale?
questions.answer: BullMQ configuration in BullMQModule: 1) lockDuration: 120000 (2 min) - exceeds max job duration (60-90s) to prevent premature stall detection, workers renew lock every lockRenewTime (30s), 2) stalledInterval: 30000 (30s) - checks for stalled jobs, jobs only marked stalled after lockDuration expires AND stalledInterval check, 3) maxStalledCount: 3 - allows transient issues before failing, 4) lazyConnect: true - app can start when Redis temporarily unavailable, 5) commandTimeout: 30000 - prevents blocking commands from hanging, 6) maxRetriesPerRequest: null - required for BullMQ workers, 7) reconnectOnError handles READONLY/ECONNRESET/ETIMEDOUT with exponential backoff (1s-30s), 8) defaultJobOptions: removeOnComplete: 100, removeOnFail: 500, attempts: 3 with exponential backoff.

questions.question: How do WebSocket progress updates flow from the inventory processing backend to connected frontend clients? Trace the complete real-time update pipeline.
questions.answer: The WebSocket progress pipeline: 1) ProgressGateway creates Socket.io namespace '/progress' with JWT authentication in handleConnection(), 2) Clients subscribe via 'subscribeToSession' message with sessionId, join session room, receive immediate current progress, 3) During processing, InventoryProcessingProcessor calls progressGateway.broadcastProgressUpdate() with ProgressUpdateDto, 4) Gateway emits 'progressUpdate' to session room via server.to(sessionId).emit(), 5) Progress includes: sessionId, processedRows, successRows, errorRows, progressPercentage, currentRate, remainingTimeSeconds, confidence, 6) On completion, broadcastSessionComplete() sends final progress, cleans up subscriptions after 30s delay, 7) Errors broadcast via broadcastSessionError(), 8) Connection state tracked in activeConnections Map and sessionSubscriptions Map.

questions.question: What is the complete data flow when a barrel event with an unknown lot variation is processed? How does the lot resolution and normalization work?
questions.answer: Lot variation resolution flow: 1) BarrelEventService receives barrelSN (e.g., '25G29AB.10.001'), 2) Serial is split into parts, potentialLotCode extracted (first two parts), 3) LotVariationService.findLotByNumberOrVariation() called - checks both Lot.lotNumber and LotVariation.variationNumber, 4) If canonical lot found with different lotNumber than input, serial is normalized: canonicalLot.lotNumber + '.' + remaining parts, 5) For bruteforce barrel creation, createBarrelByBruteforce() extracts lot code, calls lotVariationService to find canonical, 6) If no lot found, CRITICAL error 'BruteForceCreationFailed:MissingLot' logged via AndroidSyncErrorLoggerService, 7) Normalized serial used for barrel lookup/creation, ensuring all barrels link to canonical lot ID regardless of which variation was scanned.

questions.question: How does the GraphQL subscription system propagate comment updates to connected clients? Describe the PubSub pattern and filtering mechanism.
questions.answer: Comment subscription flow: 1) PubSubService wraps graphql-subscriptions PubSub, provides publish() and asyncIterableIterator(), 2) CommentResolver defines @Subscription with filter function checking entityType and entityId match, 3) When comment created/updated, service calls pubSub.publish('commentAdded', payload), 4) Subscription filter evaluates: payload.entityType === variables.entityType && payload.entityId === variables.entityId, 5) Only matching subscribers receive the update, 6) commentUpdated, commentDeleted follow same pattern, 7) mentionedInComment subscription filters by payload.mentionedUserIds?.includes(variables.userId), 8) resolve function transforms payload before sending to client.

questions.question: What happens when a barrel relocation event is processed? Trace the data flow including location validation, displacement handling, and event creation.
questions.answer: Relocation event flow in handleRelocationEvent(): 1) Extract barrelSN and locationString from data, 2) Validate locationString exists - throw BadRequestException if missing, 3) Check if holding location via isHoldingLocationString() based on 'WH' presence and dash count, 4) If storage location: parseStorageLocationString() extracts floor/warehouse/floor2/row/bay/rick/tier, 5) Lookup StorageLocation via Prisma with exact field match, 6) Check for occupying barrel via checkLocationOccupied() - if found, create displacement BarrelEvent with 'Displaced' type, clear old barrel's locationId, 7) Find or create target barrel (with lot variation normalization), 8) Update barrel.locationId (clear holdingLocationId), set disposition to 'InStorage', 9) Create Relocation BarrelEvent with eventTypeId, 10) All within serializable transaction to prevent race conditions.

questions.question: How does the safety limits system prevent runaway inventory processing jobs? Describe the circuit breaker pattern and resource monitoring.
questions.answer: Safety limits in SafetyLimitsService: 1) checkSafetyLimits() evaluates: batchNumber vs MAX_BATCHES, consecutiveEmptyBatches vs MAX_EMPTY_BATCHES, consecutiveErrors vs MAX_CONSECUTIVE_ERRORS, processingTime vs MAX_PROCESSING_TIME, 2) Returns {canContinue, reason} - processor halts if !canContinue, 3) checkResourceLimits() monitors: memory usage via process.memoryUsage(), shouldAbort if exceeds threshold, 4) Circuit breaker pattern: track consecutiveErrors in sessionMetrics Map, resetCircuitBreaker() on successful batch, 5) emergencyStop() marks session failed, cleans up resources, 6) Processor calls cleanupSessionMetrics() on completion/failure/safety limit to prevent memory leaks, 7) cleanupOldSessionMetrics() periodic cleanup removes entries older than 24 hours, 8) All violations logged to DatabaseProgressService with FATAL logLevel.

questions.question: Trace the data flow when a frontend user queries barrels with pagination. How does the GraphQL resolver interact with Prisma to return paginated results?
questions.answer: Pagination flow: 1) Frontend sends GraphQL query with first/after or last/before cursor arguments, 2) BarrelResolver receives args via @Args(), 3) Resolver calls BarrelService method which builds Prisma findMany query, 4) Prisma query includes: where clause from filters, orderBy, take (first+1 to check hasNextPage), cursor (decoded from after/before), skip (1 if cursor provided), 5) Service transforms results to Connection type with edges (node + cursor) and pageInfo (hasNextPage, hasPreviousPage, startCursor, endCursor), 6) Cursor is typically base64-encoded ID, 7) Total count fetched separately if needed via count query, 8) DataLoader may batch related entity fetches to prevent N+1.

questions.question: How does the printer automation system process print jobs? Describe the data flow from job creation to label printing.
questions.answer: Print job flow: 1) PrinterAutomationResolver mutation creates print job, publishes 'printJobCreated' via PubSub, 2) Job queued to BullMQ queue for async processing, 3) Processor fetches job data, gets printer configuration, 4) Label template rendered with barrel/lot data, 5) Print command sent to printer via appropriate protocol (ZPL, EPL, etc.), 6) Job status updated throughout: PENDING -> PROCESSING -> COMPLETED/FAILED, 7) Each status change publishes 'printJobStatusChanged' subscription, 8) Printer status changes publish 'printerStatusChanged', 9) Frontend receives real-time updates via GraphQL subscriptions using asyncIterator pattern.

questions.question: What is the complete error handling flow when an AndroidSyncInbox record fails to process? How are errors logged and what recovery mechanisms exist?
questions.answer: Error handling flow: 1) Processor catches exception in process() method, 2) Error logged to InventoryCheckStaging via updateMany: lastError, lastErrorAt, lastHeartbeat, 3) AndroidSyncErrorLoggerService.logError() persists to DeviceSyncErrorLog table with errorLevel (WARN/ERROR/CRITICAL), errorType, context, 4) BullMQ retry mechanism: job.attemptsMade tracked, up to 3 attempts with exponential backoff (type: 'exponential', delay: 2000), 5) @OnWorkerEvent('failed') logs final failure, 6) @OnWorkerEvent('stalled') warns about lock timeout, 7) Stuck detection: processingStartedAt, lastHeartbeat, bullJobId fields enable external monitoring, 8) processingAttempts incremented on each retry, 9) For fatal errors, session status set to FAILED via progressTrackingService.

questions.question: How does Trust Mode processing work after inventory upload completion? Describe the cutoff date logic and barrel nullification flow.
questions.answer: Trust Mode flow: 1) InventoryProcessingProcessor checks session.trustModeEnabled and trustModeCutoffDate after all batches complete, 2) TrustModeProcessingService.processBarrelsForTrustMode() called with sessionId, cutoffDate, description, 3) Service queries InventoryUploadStaging for resolved barrel IDs, 4) For each barrel not in uploaded set: checks lastEventDate against cutoffDate, 5) Barrels with no events after cutoff are candidates for nullification, 6) Nullification creates BarrelEvent with 'Nullified' type, clears locationId/holdingLocationId, 7) Result includes: barrelsProcessed, barrelsNullified, barrelEventsCreated, errors array, 8) Progress logged via DatabaseProgressService with correlationId, 9) Trust Mode failure doesn't fail entire session - caught and logged as ERROR.

questions.question: Describe the data flow for the Android sync diagnostics GraphQL queries. How does data flow from the database through the resolver to the frontend?
questions.answer: Diagnostics query flow: 1) Frontend sends GraphQL query (e.g., androidSyncOverview) with optional AndroidSyncDiagnosticsInput, 2) GqlDefaultAuthGuard validates authentication, 3) AndroidSyncInboxResolver parses input: converts string dates to Date objects, 4) Resolver calls AndroidSyncInboxService method (e.g., getBasicOverview), 5) Service builds Prisma query with filters: startDate, endDate, operator, deviceName, eventType, 6) Prisma executes aggregation (count, groupBy, etc.) against AndroidSyncInbox table, 7) Service returns raw results, 8) Resolver converts null to undefined for GraphQL compatibility (null vs undefined handling), 9) GraphQL serializes response per schema types (AndroidSyncOverview, etc.), 10) Frontend receives typed response.

questions.question: How does the warehouse job staging system track scan processing status? Describe the state machine and transitions.
questions.answer: Staging state machine: 1) Initial state: record created in InventoryCheckStaging with status PROCESSING, processingStartedAt set, 2) States: PROCESSING (validation in progress), READY (validation complete, awaiting review), APPROVED (human approved), COMMITTED (changes applied), REJECTED, 3) Transitions: PROCESSING->READY on successful validation, 4) hasIssues flag set based on detected issues, 5) WarehouseJob stats updated: totalScans, pendingReview, issueCount, criticalIssueCount, 6) Job auto-transitions PENDING->IN_PROGRESS on first scan, 7) Stuck detection via processingStartedAt, lastHeartbeat, processingAttempts, 8) Commit flow: READY->APPROVED->COMMITTED, creates actual BarrelEvents, 9) Issues with requiresManualReview=true block approval.

questions.question: What is the data transformation flow when a barrel Entry event includes holding vs storage location? How does the system route to different handlers?
questions.answer: Location routing flow in handleEntryOrTransferInEvent(): 1) Extract locationString from data, 2) isHoldingLocationString() checks: !hasWH OR first char not number, AND dashCount <= 5, 3) If holding location: route to createHoldingLocationEntry() with eventTypeName 'Entry', 4) Holding flow: lookup HoldingLocation by name, normalize serial via lot variation, find/create barrel, clear locationId, set holdingLocationId, disposition='InHolding', 5) If storage location: processStorageLocationEntry() called, 6) Storage flow: parseStorageLocationString() extracts 7 components, lookup StorageLocation, check occupancy, handle displacement if needed, set locationId, disposition='InStorage', 7) Both create BarrelEvent with Entry eventTypeId, 8) TransferIn events use same handler, same Entry eventTypeId.

questions.question: How does the micro-batch orchestrator improve inventory processing performance? Describe the orchestration pattern and data flow.
questions.answer: Micro-batch orchestration: 1) MicroBatchOrchestrator service manages fine-grained batch processing, 2) Jobs queued as 'process-micro-batch' type with MicroBatchJobData: sessionId, batchNumber, batchSize, startOffset, 3) Processor.processMicroBatch() delegates to orchestrator.processMicroBatch(job), 4) Orchestrator fetches specific range from staging table using offset pagination, 5) Each micro-batch processes subset of records, enabling better progress tracking, 6) Smaller batches allow more frequent progress updates and checkpoints, 7) If micro-batch fails, only that subset retries, 8) Orchestrator coordinates multiple concurrent micro-batches (controlled by concurrency limit), 9) Final aggregation combines all micro-batch results for session completion.

questions.question: Trace the complete data flow for a GraphQL mutation that creates a new barrel event from the frontend. Include authentication, validation, and persistence.
questions.answer: Mutation flow: 1) Frontend sends GraphQL mutation with Azure AD token in Authorization header, 2) NestJS GraphQL module routes to appropriate resolver, 3) GqlDefaultAuthGuard intercepts, calls AzureAdService.validateTokenAndGetUser(), 4) Token validated: decode JWT, check issuer/audience/tenant claims, get user info via Graph API or token claims, 5) @CurrentUser() decorator injects user into resolver, 6) Resolver validates input DTO via class-validator decorators, 7) Resolver calls BarrelEventService method, 8) Service wraps in Prisma.$transaction or $androidSyncTransaction for isolation, 9) Business logic: barrel lookup/creation, location validation, event creation, 10) Prisma persists to PostgreSQL, 11) Service returns created entity, 12) Resolver transforms to GraphQL type, 13) Response serialized and sent to frontend.

questions.question: How does the RabbitMQ producer service integrate with the WMS? Describe message publishing and routing patterns.
questions.answer: RabbitMQ integration: 1) RabbitmqProducerService initialized with ConfigService to get connection params, 2) Amqplib used for connection management, 3) publishMessage() accepts exchange, routingKey, message payload, 4) Messages serialized to JSON Buffer, 5) Exchange types: direct (exact routing key match), topic (pattern matching), fanout (broadcast), 6) Common exchanges: barrel-events (event fan-out), inventory-updates (processing notifications), 7) Consumers (separate services/workers) bind queues to exchanges, 8) Delivery guarantees via confirms and acks, 9) Dead letter exchange handles failed messages, 10) Used for decoupled communication: audit logging, external integrations, async notifications.

questions.question: What is the complete flow when matching suggestions are generated for a BAD_QR scan? How does the barrel matching service work?
questions.answer: Match suggestion flow: 1) InventoryCheckStagingProcessor detects BAD_QR pattern in validateAndDetectIssues(), 2) CRITICAL issue created with errorType 'BadQrScan:RequiresEandTIntervention', 3) BarrelMatchingService.findMatches() called with locationString, warehouseJobId, barrelSerialNumber, 4) Service queries barrels near location - uses warehouse/floor/row proximity, 5) Filters by lot patterns if partial data available in jsonEntityPayload, 6) Ranks matches by: exact location match, same row, same floor, lot similarity, 7) Returns MatchSuggestion[] with barrelId, serialNumber, confidence score, reason, 8) Issue.context updated with matchSuggestions array, 9) Frontend displays suggestions for E&T team to select correct barrel.

questions.question: How does the feature flags service control barrel re-entry mode? Trace the data flow from configuration to runtime behavior.
questions.answer: Feature flag flow: 1) FeatureFlagsService injected into BarrelEventService, 2) FEATURE_FLAG_KEYS.BARREL_REENTRY_MODE defines flag key, 3) featureFlags.get<BarrelReentryMode>(key) fetches current value, 4) Values: 'strict' (error on duplicate location), 'warn' (log but allow), 'silent' (no action), 5) Flag values stored in database or config, cached with TTL, 6) In handleEntryOrTransferInEvent(), checks reentryMode before processing, 7) If barrel already has location and mode='strict', throws ConflictException, 8) If mode='warn', logs via AndroidSyncErrorLoggerService but continues, 9) Dynamic toggle allows runtime behavior change without deployment, 10) Used for gradual rollout and A/B testing.

questions.question: Describe the data flow for reconciliation updates via GraphQL subscriptions. How are approvals propagated to connected clients?
questions.answer: Reconciliation subscription flow: 1) ReconciliationResolver defines @Subscription for reconciliationUpdates and approvalUpdates, 2) reconciliationUpdates uses filter function that can match subscription variables, 3) When reconciliation record updated, service publishes to pubSub with trigger 'RECONCILIATION_UPDATED', 4) Payload includes full ReconciliationConnectionType, 5) approvalUpdates subscription listens on 'APPROVAL_UPDATED' trigger, 6) Approval workflow: submit -> pending_review -> approved/rejected, 7) Each state change publishes event, 8) Clients receive real-time updates via WebSocket transport, 9) Apollo Client subscription handler updates local cache, 10) UI re-renders with new data.

questions.question: How does the database progress service log processing events? Describe the event logging data model and query patterns.
questions.answer: Progress logging flow: 1) DatabaseProgressService provides startBatchProgress(), updateBatchProgress(), completeBatchProgress(), 2) startBatchProgress() creates InventoryProcessingProgress record with sessionId, batchNumber, startedAt, 3) logProcessingEvent() creates InventoryProcessingLog with: sessionId, batchNumber, logLevel (INFO/WARN/ERROR/FATAL), logCategory (processing/checkpoint/error/performance), message, details JSON, correlationId, 4) updateBatchProgress() updates counts: processedCount, successCount, errorCount, skippedCount, checkpoint data, 5) updateBatchResourceMetrics() tracks memory/CPU usage, 6) Queries: filter by sessionId, batchNumber, logLevel, dateRange, 7) Used for debugging, audit trail, performance analysis, 8) correlationId links related events across batches.

questions.question: What is the complete flow when a withdrawal/transfer-out event is processed? How does the barrel disposition change?
questions.answer: Withdrawal flow in handleWithdrawalOrTransferOutEvent(): 1) Extract barrelSN, locationString, operator from data, 2) Find barrel by serialNumber (with lot variation normalization), 3) If barrel not found, throw NotFoundException, 4) Validate withdrawal payload if present (class-validator), 5) Clear barrel's locationId AND holdingLocationId (exits warehouse), 6) Set disposition to 'Withdrawn' or 'TransferredOut', 7) Lookup 'Withdrawal' eventType by name, 8) Create BarrelEvent with: barrelId, eventTypeId, eventTime, createdBy, notes from jsonEntityPayload, 9) For TransferOut, same flow but different disposition, 10) Returns created barrelEvent, storageLocation undefined (barrel no longer at location).

questions.question: How does the audit dashboard subscription system deliver real-time audit log updates? Describe the filtering and data transformation.
questions.answer: Audit subscription flow: 1) AuditDashboardResolver defines @Subscription with nullable return, 2) Filter function checks variables.filter against payload: if no filter, returns true (all events), 3) Can filter by: entityType, entityId, userId, action type, date range, 4) When audit event created (via AuditLogService), publishes to trigger, 5) Payload is AuditLogConnection with edges and pageInfo, 6) Filter evaluates each incoming event against subscription variables, 7) Only matching events delivered to client, 8) Client receives structured audit log with: id, entityType, entityId, action, userId, timestamp, changes (before/after diff), 9) Used for compliance monitoring, real-time activity feeds.

questions.question: Trace the data flow for inventory check job completion. How do scans transition from staging to committed barrel events?
questions.answer: Job completion flow: 1) All scans in InventoryCheckStaging reach READY status, 2) User reviews issues, resolves CRITICAL ones (BAD_QR mapping, etc.), 3) Commit mutation called on WarehouseJob, 4) Service iterates READY staging records, 5) For each: creates BarrelEvent via BarrelEventService, 6) Event creation follows same logic as direct sync: location validation, displacement handling, 7) Staging status updated to COMMITTED, 8) WarehouseJob status set to COMPLETED, endedAt timestamp set, 9) Any staging with unresolved CRITICAL issues blocks commit, 10) Commit wrapped in transaction for atomicity, 11) Stats updated: committedCount, errorCount, 12) WebSocket notification for completion.

questions.question: How does the CachedEventTypeService optimize event type lookups? Describe the caching strategy and its impact on transaction performance.
questions.answer: EventType caching: 1) CachedEventTypeService caches EventType records to avoid in-transaction queries (TEC-fix), 2) Cache populated on module init or first access, 3) getEventTypeByName() checks cache first, returns cached EventType if found, 4) Cache invalidation on EventType CRUD operations, 5) In BarrelEventService, injected as cachedEventTypeService, 6) Used in createBarrelEventWithLocationAndLocationCheck handlers, 7) Eliminates need for: transactionClient.eventType.findFirst() inside transaction, 8) Reduces transaction duration, preventing lock contention, 9) EventTypes are reference data, rarely change, ideal for caching, 10) TTL-based refresh or manual invalidation on admin updates.

questions.question: What is the data flow when processing an inspection event? How are repair reasons captured and persisted?
questions.answer: Inspection event flow in handleInspectionEvent(): 1) Extract barrelSN, locationString, operator, jsonEntityPayload from data, 2) Find barrel by serialNumber, throw if not found, 3) Validate jsonEntityPayload as InspectionPayload using class-validator: requires repairReason string, 4) Lookup 'Inspection' eventType, 5) Create BarrelEvent with: barrelId, eventTypeId, eventTime, createdBy, notes containing stringified payload with repairReason, 6) Barrel location unchanged (inspection doesn't move barrel), 7) InspectionPayload class defines validation: @IsString() repairReason, 8) Validation errors throw BadRequestException with details, 9) Returns barrelEvent and current location info.

questions.question: How does the JWT service integrate with the WebSocket progress gateway for authentication? Describe the token validation flow.
questions.answer: WebSocket JWT auth flow: 1) ProgressGateway.handleConnection() receives Socket with handshake data, 2) Token extracted from: client.handshake.auth?.token OR client.handshake.headers?.authorization?.replace('Bearer ', ''), 3) If no token: emit 'error' and disconnect, 4) JwtService.verifyAsync(token) validates signature and expiration, 5) Payload extracted: { sub: userId, ... }, 6) If !payload?.sub: emit 'error', disconnect, 7) On success: client.userId = payload.sub, client.sessionIds = new Set(), 8) Client tracked in activeConnections Map, 9) 'connected' event emitted with socketId and timestamp, 10) Subsequent messages use authenticated context.

questions.question: Trace the complete data flow for EMPTY_POSITION scan processing. How does the system validate empty locations and handle existing barrels?
questions.answer: EMPTY_POSITION flow: 1) Processor detects EMPTY_POSITION_* pattern via isEmptyPositionScan() regex, 2) Resolves location via resolveLocationFromString(), 3) If location found: determines type (storage vs holding), 4) For storage: checkLocationOccupied() finds any barrel with that locationId, 5) For holding: queries barrel with holdingLocationId match, 6) If occupying barrel found: creates WARN issue 'EmptyPositionValidation:BarrelWillBeDisplaced' with context including displaced barrel info, resolutionAction='DISPLACE', 7) If no barrel: creates INFO issue 'EmptyPositionValidation:LocationConfirmedEmpty', resolutionAction='VALIDATE_EMPTY', 8) If location not found: CRITICAL issue 'EmptyPositionValidation:LocationNotFound', 9) Skips all barrel validation phases, saves issues, updates staging.

questions.question: How does the batch continuation logic work in inventory processing? Describe the decision tree for queuing follow-up batches.
questions.answer: Continuation logic in processInventorySession(): 1) After processBatch() returns batchResult, 2) checkForPendingRecords() queries staging: count records with status IN ('pending', 'validated'), 3) Checkpoint from batchResult provides remainingWork.totalRemaining, 4) shouldContinue = batchResult.processedCount > 0 OR stillHasPending > 0 OR checkpoint.remainingWork.totalRemaining > 0, 5) If shouldContinue: queue new job via processingQueue.add('process-inventory-session', {sessionId, batchSize}), 6) Job options: attempts: 3, exponential backoff, removeOnComplete: true, 7) Progress set to <100%, completeBatchProgress with continuationQueued: true, 8) If !shouldContinue: session complete flow - 100% progress, completeProcessingTracking(), Trust Mode if enabled, cleanup sessionMetrics.

questions.question: What is the data flow when a DumpForRegauge event is processed? How does this differ from other barrel events?
questions.answer: DumpForRegauge flow in handleDumpForRegaugeEvent(): 1) Extract barrelSN, operator, jsonEntityPayload from data, 2) Find barrel by serialNumber (throws if not found), 3) Lookup 'DumpForRegauge' eventType by name, 4) Special handling: barrel disposition may change to indicate regauge pending, 5) Location may be cleared or set to special regauge holding area, 6) Create BarrelEvent with eventTypeId, notes containing regauge context, 7) Differs from standard events: triggers downstream regauge workflow, 8) May create linked records for regauge tracking, 9) Returns barrelEvent with updated barrel state, 10) Business process: barrel contents dumped for re-measurement/re-grading.

questions.question: How does the Verified event type work? Describe the data flow and payload validation.
